importNotes: 36 of 48 planned v4 core corpus documents are registered here: 28 confirmed documents from the current lincoln-metaphor-analysis corpus_manifest.json (all at pipeline_stage_completed: 4) and 8 additional documents identified in LMA issue #113 as v4 priority additions not yet in the source-repo manifest. The remaining 12 documents needed to reach the 48-doc v4 target have not yet been specified in the source repository (LMA #113 is still open). This inventory will be updated when the child-repo v4 corpus expansion is complete. Provenance source: ashitaka-emishi/lincoln-metaphor-analysis.

documentTiers.description: Fully annotated core set; receives passage segmentation, claim extraction, and scoring. 28 documents are confirmed (pipeline_stage_completed: 4 in source manifest); 8 are planned v4 additions from LMA #113 not yet in manifest; 12 remain unspecified.

Lighter annotation tier for inter-annotator reliability sampling and recurrence/negative-finding testing. Includes all v4-core documents (36 registered in PPW #195) plus 39-64 additional documents selected from the candidate category pools below. The categories are overlapping candidate pools (estimated aggregate 45-69 documents); not all categories need be represented and no single category must be drawn to its stated maximum. Final selection must keep total additional count within 39-64 (total tier within 75-100). Specific document selection awaits LMA #114 resolution.

tierDefinition.ppwAnnotationScope: passage-level segmentation only; no claim extraction, no scoring
tierDefinition.vsCoreTier: Core tier receives full annotation pipeline (passages → claims → interpretations → scores). Validation tier receives passage segmentation only, enabling reliability sampling without requiring full claim extraction.
tierDefinition.reliabilityUse: Stratified sample from validation tier used for inter-annotator reliability study (PPW #199). Annotators code passages independently; agreement measured against core-tier adjudicated annotations.
tierDefinition.negativeUse: Validation tier documents outside the core set test for recurrence (does metaphor cluster appear in less-studied documents?) and negative findings (documents in the same genre or period where cluster is absent).

candidateCategories.description: Annual messages to Congress not in the core set (December 1863, December 1864)
candidateCategories.rationale: Complete the administrative-register chronological series; diachronic tracking of obligatory frame in formal reports

candidateCategories.description: Thanksgiving and national day proclamations (1861-1865); Proclamation of Amnesty and Reconstruction (December 8, 1863) and related proclamations. Note: avoid double-counting with reconstruction-documents category — the Proclamation of Amnesty should be counted in only one category.
candidateCategories.rationale: Legal/ritual register; test whether covenant and sacrifice metaphors appear in formal proclamation genre or are suppressed as in Emancipation Proclamations

candidateCategories.description: Selected general orders and military correspondence with Union commanders
candidateCategories.rationale: Military administrative register; negative-finding test for metaphor suppression in command prose

candidateCategories.description: Public letters on slavery, Union, race, and war powers not in core set
candidateCategories.rationale: Extends public-letter genre coverage; tracks covenant-over-freedom cluster across multiple correspondents

candidateCategories.description: Private letters on slavery, providence, and sacrifice not in core set
candidateCategories.rationale: Extends private-register coverage; tests whether public rhetoric maps onto private cognition across a larger sample

candidateCategories.description: Letters and orders related to Black soldier recruitment and emancipation implementation (1863-1865)
candidateCategories.rationale: Tests distribution of obligatory-frame and body-organic clusters across racial contexts

candidateCategories.description: Lincoln's correspondence and speeches on colonization (1862)
candidateCategories.rationale: Politically sensitive cluster; tests for metaphor suppression or activation in colonization rhetoric

candidateCategories.description: Letters of condolence to bereaved families of Union soldiers. Authorship caution: the Bixby Letter (November 21, 1864) is the most prominent condolence letter but authorship is disputed (possible John Hay attribution); apply authorship confidence controls analogous to Seward passages in lma-doc-009 if included.
candidateCategories.rationale: Wound-and-healing and sacrifice clusters in highly personal private register; tests unguarded theodicy expression

candidateCategories.description: Reconstruction policy documents and correspondence not in core set (e.g., Wade-Davis Pocket Veto message, Louisiana reconstruction correspondence, letters to governors on restoration). Note: avoid double-counting Proclamation of Amnesty and Reconstruction with the proclamations category.
candidateCategories.rationale: Diachronic endpoint for organism cluster beyond Last Public Address; tracks post-war body-politic frame

documentTiers.notes: The 36 documents registered in the core tier (#195) are implicitly included in the validation tier — they receive validation-level annotation (passage segmentation) as a subset of their full core-tier processing. This `currentCount: 0` reflects additional-only documents beyond the core set that have not yet been individually selected. Specific document selection requires LMA #114 to be resolved in the child repository. Validation tier documents will be added to this documents[] array when LMA #114 closes.

documentTiers.description: Exhaustive contextual corpus indexed for keyword and context search only. No annotation at any level. Includes all core and validation tier documents plus additional Lincoln and contemporaneous documents that provide search context without adding annotation burden. Target count is open-ended (upper bound: complete Lincoln Collected Works plus key contemporaneous documents). Specific document selection awaits LMA #115 resolution.

tierDefinition.ppwAnnotationScope: search indexing only; no passage segmentation, no claim extraction, no scoring
tierDefinition.vsCoreTier: Core tier receives full annotation pipeline (passages → claims → interpretations → scores). Reference tier is indexed only — documents are retrievable and citable but carry no annotation structure.
tierDefinition.vsValidationTier: Validation tier receives passage segmentation for reliability sampling. Reference tier receives no annotation at all — not even passage boundaries.
tierDefinition.searchUse: Reference-tier documents enable contextual search across a broader Lincoln corpus: keyword queries, inter-document navigation, and negative-space checks (confirming a metaphor cluster is absent in a given register or period).
tierDefinition.annotationExclusion: Reference tier documents must NOT be promoted to claim candidates or used as the basis for scoring without explicit upgrade to validation or core tier.

candidateCategories.description: Lincoln Collected Works documents not in core or validation tiers — campaign speeches, lesser-known addresses, minor correspondence
candidateCategories.rationale: Exhaustive Lincoln coverage for search; ensures no significant document is outside the search index

candidateCategories.description: House floor speeches and committee remarks (1847-1849 congressional term) not in core or validation sets
candidateCategories.rationale: Pre-Peoria legislative register; tests for metaphor suppression or activation in formal legislative discourse

candidateCategories.description: Select legal briefs, circuit-court arguments, and law-practice documents (1836-1860)
candidateCategories.rationale: Legal register; tests whether persuasive legal argument activates or suppresses the rhetorical clusters present in political addresses

candidateCategories.description: 1858 campaign materials and 1860 campaign documents not already in core or validation tiers
candidateCategories.rationale: Electoral-register documents; diachronic bridge between Peoria and the presidential period

candidateCategories.description: Selected documents by contemporaries (Douglas, Chase, Seward, Frederick Douglass) for comparative-context search
candidateCategories.rationale: Non-Lincoln documents for negative-space and contrast searches; must be marked authorId ≠ Lincoln and excluded from any Lincoln-specific metaphor scoring
candidateCategories.authorshipNote: These documents are NOT Lincoln-authored. Use authorId and corpusTier fields to prevent accidental inclusion in Lincoln-metaphor scoring runs.

candidateCategories.description: Contemporary newspaper transcriptions of Lincoln speeches where no authoritative Collected Works text exists
candidateCategories.rationale: Fills gaps where CW has no text; must carry editorialStatus: uncertain and sourceAuthority: newspaper-transcript
candidateCategories.textQualityNote: Transcription fidelity varies; flag with riskFlags: [transcription-uncertainty] and do not use as primary text for any annotation work.

documentTiers.notes: The core (36 registered) and validation (75-100 total) tier documents are implicitly included in the reference index — all annotated documents are also searchable. This `currentCount: 0` reflects additional-only reference documents beyond the validation set that have not yet been individually selected. targetCount is null (open-ended): the reference corpus grows to encompass whatever documents are useful for contextual search without a fixed ceiling. Contemporaneous-context documents (non-Lincoln) must be clearly marked to prevent inclusion in metaphor-scoring runs. Specific document selection requires LMA #115 to be resolved in the child repository.

documents.title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Earliest major speech. Proto-covenant and proto-organism metaphors before Lincoln has political stakes — valuable baseline.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Temperance Address
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Body/disease cluster in alcohol reform context — establishes pre-political use of organic metaphors.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Eulogy on Henry Clay
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Ancestral-debt cluster. Lincoln positions Clay as exemplar of covenant-keeping.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Speech at Peoria, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: First major deployment of experiment/proposition frame re: slavery. 17,000 words.
knownLimitations: Reported or extemporaneous text may contain transcription noise; exact wording and span boundaries require caution.

documents.title: House Divided Speech
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Body-organism cluster at full intensity. Dual activation: house = body AND household covenant.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: First Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Ottawa, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Metaphors under adversarial pressure. Annotate Lincoln turns only.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Second Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Freeport, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Proposition cluster under maximum argumentative pressure.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Third Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Jonesboro, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Southern IL audience — watch for metaphor suppression vs. northern venues.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Fourth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Charleston, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: CRITICAL for absence analysis: Lincoln suppresses equality-proposition cluster here. Tag explicitly.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Fifth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Galesburg, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Compare cluster activation with Jonesboro (suppressed) and Galesburg (receptive).
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Sixth Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Quincy, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Included as the sixth Lincoln-Douglas debate for full debate-series coverage and register-controlled comparison.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Seventh Lincoln-Douglas Debate, Alton, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Transcribed from newspaper accounts; annotate Lincoln turns only.
documents.selectionRationale: Final debate — Lincoln's closing synthesis after 7 rounds of adversarial pressure.
knownLimitations: Debate text depends on nineteenth-century newspaper transcriptions; exact wording and span boundaries require caution. Annotation and analysis are limited to Lincoln turns.

documents.title: Address at Cooper Institute, New York City
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Best single document for fathers/inheritance cluster. Lincoln cites founders by name.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Farewell Address at Springfield, Illinois
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Two versions exist: extemporaneous transcription and Lincoln's written reconstruction. Analyze both.
documents.selectionRationale: Providence cluster in unguarded form. Lincoln speaking extemporaneously about his own death.
knownLimitations: Two textual versions circulate; variant-sensitive claims should identify the version used.

documents.title: First Inaugural Address
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Seward-origin/revised closing peroration. Use three tiers: received text, Lincoln-adopted Seward-origin wording, and strict Lincoln-origin evidence.
documents.selectionRationale: Retain as a Lincoln-primary inaugural address while applying sentence-level Seward controls: received text, Lincoln-adopted wording, and strict Lincoln-origin evidence.
knownLimitations: Includes Seward-origin/revised closing passages; authorship-sensitive analysis should caveat inst_00076 and exclude inst_00077 from Lincoln-sole or strict Lincoln-origin claims.

documents.title: Message to Congress in Special Session, July 4, 1861
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Experiment/proposition most explicit here. Note how metaphors operate in administrative register.
knownLimitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

documents.title: Fragment on the Constitution and the Union
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Date uncertain; probably January 1861. Private note with no intended audience.
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln privately reasons Union is 'picture of silver', liberty the 'apple of gold'. Rare explicit metaphor theorizing. High epistemic value.
knownLimitations: Private fragment with uncertain date; diachronic placement should be treated cautiously.
Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

documents.title: Letter to Horace Greeley
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: CRITICAL. Metaphor suppression event. Written 2 months before Preliminary Emancipation. Track covenant-over-freedom suppression.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Preliminary Emancipation Proclamation
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Deliberately flat. Absence of birth/body clusters is the finding. Use as control document.
knownLimitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

documents.title: Annual Message to Congress, December 1, 1862
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: 'Last best hope of earth' — experiment frame at high intensity in administrative register.
knownLimitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density and should be controlled before comparing to formal addresses.

documents.title: Emancipation Proclamation
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Compare to preliminary — same flatness. The document actually freeing enslaved people contains almost no metaphor. Flatness is data.
knownLimitations: Legal register intentionally suppresses metaphor; absence findings should be interpreted as register-controlled evidence, not as source failure.

documents.title: Letter to James C. Conkling
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Written to be read aloud at rally. Defends emancipation to war Democrats. Track obligatory frame distribution across racial lines.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.authorshipNotes: Use Bliss copy as authoritative text.
documents.selectionRationale: 272 words. Birth, proposition, sacrifice, ancestral-debt all present. Highest metaphor density. Use Bliss copy.
knownLimitations: Multiple manuscript witnesses exist; variant-sensitive claims should identify the copy used.

documents.title: Memorandum Concerning His Probable Failure of Re-election
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Private note sealed from cabinet. Providence cluster in purely private form. Key test: does public providence rhetoric reflect genuine private cognition?
knownLimitations: Private fragment has limited audience context and short length; frequency claims require caution.

documents.title: Letter to Albert G. Hodges
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: 'I claim not to have controlled events but confess plainly that events have controlled me.' Obligatory frame canonical. Also proto-Second-Inaugural theodicy.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Response to a Serenade, November 10, 1864
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Extemporaneous. Experiment/proof frame unguarded — election result as empirical evidence.
knownLimitations: Reported or extemporaneous text may contain transcription noise; exact wording and span boundaries require caution.

documents.title: Second Inaugural Address
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: All clusters present. Providence/theodicy at full development. Distributed guilt. Obligatory frame at maximum. Diachronic endpoint.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: Last Public Address
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Reconstruction policy. Wound-and-healing now programmatic. Diachronic endpoint for organism cluster. Delivered 4 days before assassination.
knownLimitations: No special provenance caution recorded.

documents.title: First Political Announcement
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln's first public document (age 23). Earliest baseline for political voice before covenant and organism metaphors develop. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Letter to George Robertson
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln's private pessimism about peaceful resolution — 'blood will flow' — written ten months after Peoria as Lincoln processes the limits of argument. Covenant-breaking frame in private register. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Letter to Joshua F. Speed
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Nine days after Robertson letter. Lincoln explains his moral position on slavery to a slaveowner friend. Nation-as-body frame in private register — highly candid. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Speech on the Dred Scott Decision
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln's direct response to Taney's ruling. Proposition and experiment frames deployed against constitutional regression. Critical link between Peoria and Douglas debates. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Reported speech text may contain transcription noise.
Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Address at Independence Hall, Philadelphia
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln publicly ties himself to the Declaration of Independence days before taking office — willingness to die for the promise. Birth/covenant cluster at maximum intensity in compressed form. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Reported speech; transcription noise possible.
Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Annual Message to Congress, December 3, 1861
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: First wartime annual message. Compare with July 4, 1861 special message and December 1862 message for diachronic administrative-register metaphor tracking. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Administrative register constrains metaphor density; control before comparing to formal addresses.
Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Letter to Erastus Corning and Others
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln's extended defense of executive war powers and suspension of habeas corpus. Necessity/emergency frame under direct challenge. Important for constitutional-covenant cluster outside speech genre. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.

documents.title: Meditation on the Divine Will
documents.author: Abraham Lincoln
documents.sourceEdition: Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln, University of Michigan digital edition
documents.selectionRationale: Lincoln's private theodicy — written for himself alone, probably September 1862. Purest expression of the obligatory-frame: God controls outcomes, Lincoln is instrument. Proto-Second-Inaugural in compressed form. Rare unmediated access to Lincoln's private metaphysical reasoning. Required addition from LMA v4 corpus expansion (LMA #113).
knownLimitations: Date uncertain; dated 'September 1862' by editors based on content proximity to Emancipation Proclamation decision.
Private fragment has limited audience context; frequency claims require caution.
Not yet added to source-repo corpus_manifest.json; LMA #113 open.
